Name: CurvatureAwarePrecisionRouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Neural Network Training
Short Hypothesis: Curvature-aware precision routing can dynamically adjust numerical precision during training, enhancing both computational efficiency and numerical stability in neural networks.
Related Work: Recent works such as 'Curvature-aware dynamic precision approach for physics-informed neural networks' and 'Tri-Accel: Curvature-Aware Precision-Adaptive and Memory-Elastic Optimization for Efficient GPU Usage' have explored the use of curvature information to adapt numerical precision during training. However, these approaches focus primarily on specific applications or do not fully integrate curvature diagnostics with deterministic, algorithmically grounded optimization proxies. Our proposal aims to bridge this gap by developing a curvature-aware precision routing framework that tightly couples geometric subspace diagnostics with deterministic optimization, ensuring strict computational overhead bounds and explicit numerical stability analysis.
Abstract: We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for neural network training that dynamically adjusts numerical precision based on curvature diagnostics. This approach integrates spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, ensuring strict computational overhead bounds and preserving deterministic execution pathways. By quantifying gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, our method replaces heuristic fragility claims with formalized stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. We validate the framework through empirical evaluations on standard benchmarks, demonstrating significant improvements in training efficiency and numerical stability.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on training efficiency and numerical stability.
Experiments.Method: Train neural networks on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets using the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework. Compare the training time, memory usage, and accuracy with static precision (FP32 and FP64) and existing mixed-precision methods. Use metrics such as training time, memory consumption, and accuracy to quantify improvements.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, accuracy, and precision switching frequency.

Experiments.Description: Analyze the curvature diagnostics and their impact on precision switching.
Experiments.Method: Collect and analyze curvature diagnostics (gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement) during training. Visualize these metrics to understand their correlation with precision switching decisions. Use this analysis to refine the precision routing algorithm.
Experiments.Metrics: Curvature diagnostics, precision switching frequency, and training stability.

Experiments.Description: Conduct a multi-seed variance quantification study.
Experiments.Method: Train the model multiple times with different random seeds to evaluate the consistency and robustness of the curvature-aware precision routing. Analyze the variance in training time, memory usage, and accuracy across different runs.
Experiments.Metrics: Multi-seed variance in training time, memory usage, and accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Compare with state-of-the-art mixed-precision training methods.
Experiments.Method: Implement and evaluate state-of-the-art mixed-precision training methods (e.g., NVIDIA's automatic mixed precision) on the same datasets and tasks. Compare the performance of these methods with the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, accuracy, and precision switching frequency.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The precision switching algorithm may introduce additional computational overhead, which could negate some of the efficiency gains.
The curvature diagnostics may not always accurately reflect the true numerical stability requirements, leading to suboptimal precision switching decisions.
The framework's performance may vary across different neural network architectures and datasets, requiring further validation and tuning.
The method relies on deterministic spectral estimators, which may not capture all forms of numerical instability.
